items.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think: Advancing science, research and engineering
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has published a wave of significant research in early 2026. Most notably, the Aletheia AI research agent was introduced in March 2026 — a mathematics research agent that goes beyond competition-level problem solving to autonomously generate, verify, and revise mathematical proofs, having produced an entire research paper (Feng26) in arithmetic geometry without human intervention. Alongside this, DeepMind released a paper titled 'Measuring Progress Towards AGI: A Cognitive Framewo

items.title: Clarification on Stable Replacement Models for gemini-2.5-flash and gemini-2.5-pro Before June 2026 Deprecation
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: As of March 2026, the Gemini API has seen significant updates across new model releases, feature launches, and deprecations. On the model side, Google released Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview as the latest flagship in the Gemini 3 series, alongside Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview (the first Flash-Lite in the Gemini 3 family) and Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (also branded as Nano Banana 2), optimized for high-speed, high-volume image generation at 4K resolution. A specialized endpoint gemini-3.1-pro-previ

items.title: Get started with a Firebase Studio template - Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet:
Firebase Studio is Google's agentic, cloud-based development environment designed for building full-stack and AI-powered applications, including games. It integrates Gemini AI as an always-available assistant, supports frameworks like p5.js, and provides emulators, testing frameworks, and one-click deployment to Firebase Hosting. The platform offers a range of templates to accelerate project setup and supports importing from GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket.

For game development specifically, Googl

Alongside this, DeepMind released a paper titled 'Measuring Progress Towards AGI: A Cognitive Framewo

items.title: Genkit Overview - Open-source AI development framework by Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is an open-source AI framework by Google available in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, designed to help developers build, test, and deploy generative AI features into production apps. The official documentation covers a full getting-started flow including project setup, installing packages, configuring API keys (e.g., Gemini via Google AI), creating your first AI flow, and testing locally via the Genkit Developer UI. Genkit supports a wide range of model providers including

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now in public preview in GitHub Copilot - GitHub Changelog
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet:
The google-gemini GitHub organization has been active with significant releases in early 2026. The flagship project, gemini-cli, has been releasing nightly builds regularly, with the most recent version reaching 0.36.0-nightly.20260317 as of March 2026. Key milestones include Plan Mode being enabled by default as of v0.34.0 (March 17, 2026), tool isolation and sandboxing with Linux bubblewrap/seccomp support, and integration with the new Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview model.

On the model side, Gemini 3

items.title: Google Antigravity - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Google Antigravity, Google's agentic development platform launched through AI Studio, has continued receiving updates into early 2026. The platform enables developers to operate at a higher, task-oriented level — acting as architects who collaborate with intelligent agents that autonomously manage the editor, terminal, and browser. Key capabilities include full-stack vibe coding (turning prompts into production-ready apps with React, Angular, or Next.js), automatic Firebase integration for datab

items.title: Genkit - Open-source AI framework by Google | Firebase
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework by Google (part of Firebase) for building full-stack AI-powered applications using familiar code-centric patterns. It supports JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, and provides primitives for flows (observable, typed AI functions), tool use, RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), and multi-agent orchestration. The framework is designed to make it easy to develop, integrate, test, and deploy AI features with built-in observability and evaluations, and it wor

items.title: Design UI using AI with Stitch from Google Labs
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google AI Studio launched a major full-stack 'vibe coding' experience in early-to-mid 2026, allowing developers to build production-ready applications from plain English prompts. The platform integrates an AI agent called Antigravity that acts as a project manager and code agent — it plans the project, writes code across multiple files, installs dependencies, runs tests, catches errors, and iterates autonomously. The default output is a full-stack environment with a React frontend and a Node.js

items.title: Gemini Drops: New updates to the Gemini app, January 2026
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google has been actively releasing major Gemini AI updates in early 2026. Most recently, Gemini 3.1 Pro launched in preview with higher usage limits for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers, and is now available on NotebookLM for those tiers. Workspace integrations received new Gemini features in beta for Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive, rolling out first to Ultra and Pro plan users globally (English) and in the U.S. for Drive. The Gemini app also received monthly 'Gemini Drops' update packages i

items.title: Gemini for Government: Supporting U.S. Government's AI Transformation | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several major Gemini AI announcements in early 2026. Most notably, Gemini 3.1 Pro was launched in preview on Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise, offering significantly improved reasoning and problem-solving capabilities over the Gemini 3 baseline. It is also accessible via the Gemini API in Google AI Studio, Android Studio, and Gemini CLI. Alongside it, Google introduced Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite — the fastest and most cost-efficient model in the Gemini 3 series, targeting high-vo

items.title: OpenAI compatibility | Gemini API | Google AI for Developers
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API provides two complementary mechanisms for obtaining structured data from models: native structured output (JSON Schema enforcement) and function calling. Structured output allows developers to configure Gemini models to generate responses that strictly adhere to a provided JSON Schema, ensuring type-safe, predictable results. This is particularly useful for extracting structured data from unstructured text. Google GenAI SDKs support schema definition via Pydantic (Python) and Zod

items.title: Google DeepMind on X — Project Genie Rollout
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind and Google AI have been active on social media in early-to-mid 2026 with several major announcements. Key highlights include the announcement of Genie 3, a general-purpose world model capable of generating dynamic, interactive 3D environments from a single text prompt, representing a significant leap from Genie 2 with improved real-time interactivity and consistency. Google DeepMind also announced that Project Genie is rolling out to Google AI Ultra subscribers in the U.S. (18+),
